figures.caption: Figure 4: Experimental results with different execution sequences. Figure (a) and (b) show the experimental results of lower strategies with different execution orders on HalfCheetah 3 × 2 and Walker2d 3 × 2, respectively. Figure (c) and (d) show the different execution sequences of the STEP algorithm in these two environments.
Figure 5: The mean episode rewards obtained by the proposed HPA and benchmarks.
Figure 6: Data characteristics. Mean denotes the mean episode rewards and max denotes the max episode rewards.
Figure 8: Influence of the k for HAP

Figure 7: Visualisation experiments. (a) is the comparison experimental results of HPA and lower strategy. (b) is the experimental results showing different execution sequences of lower strategies. (c) and (d) are the visualisation experiments of sequential selection under random seeds of 1 and 10 after training.
Figure 3: The overall architecture of HPA.
